content:
<@1131790839291596841> <@1488457102635565179> 
Not sure if this is the right channel for this but, just wanted to share.

Regarding the Korean translation, I feel there's a lot of confusion among
translators — both official and volunteer. I'd like to suggest a few process
improvements to help address this.

# The Biggest Issues

1. **Inconsistency in character names and speech styles**
   (반말/존댓말 distinction, like mixing formal and informal speech within
   a single character's lines)

2. **Story translation**
   - Overall quality has improved quite a lot, but Season 1 Chapter 5
     is in rough shape right now.

## Why Does This Matter?

- There was a significant influx of new Korean users last month, and many
  had a hard time immersing themselves in the main story due to these
  inconsistencies.
- There was also an incident where volunteer translations were overwritten
  by a GPT version for the earlier part of the 2.5 anniversary event,
  which was quite damaging.

# Suggested Solutions

## 1. A Work Progress Tracking Spreadsheet

### Layout

- **Rows:** Categories requiring translation
  - Main Story S1C1 – S2C5 and onward | Side stories |
    Character-specific lines | Card text | Rules | etc.
- **Columns:** Current status, Assignee, Progress, Last updated
  - Status and Assignee should each distinguish between:
    GPT | Volunteer | Official

### Usage

This sheet should be shared across all translators and the dev team
to keep everyone aligned on progress.

---

## 2. A Glossary Sheet

A shared glossary is essential so all translators are working from
the same reference — especially for character names, skill names,
and effect names.

---

## 3. Prioritize the Main Story — Especially Consistency

Before expanding coverage, it would be worth locking down consistent
character names and speech styles in the main story first.

---

## 4. A Dedicated Coordinator — and Clear Ownership (IMPORTANT)

I'm not sure whether this should fall on the official translator or
someone on the dev team, but there needs to be a clear central point
of coordination for assigning and distributing work. Without visibility
into what the official translator is currently handling, volunteers will
have difficulty deciding where to focus — and overlap or gaps become
inevitable.

More broadly, someone needs to take ownership of Korean translation as
a whole — otherwise this will only get harder to manage.

- If the official translator is embedded within the team, it would be
  worth formally empowering that person and giving them clear ownership.
- If they're working remotely or as an outsourced contractor, it becomes
  even more critical that someone internal holds that role and acts as
  the point of contact.

In either case, the person in charge should have direct access to the
PM or dev team to verify what's actually been applied to the build —
because that visibility is essential for coordinating everything else.

---

# Final Words

I don't mean to overstep — I've worked in similar environments before,
and I know firsthand how difficult this kind of coordination can be for
a small team. I hope this is at least somewhat helpful.

I made a mock up spreadsheet in case it helps.
<@1131790839291596841> <@1488457102635565179> 🙏
"Keeper" gets misspelled as "Kiiper" when talking to Jenkins on the Communicator in one of their chats.

Hi Kiiper! It's Jenkin is making a typo because's she's a little illiterate; she typed 守咪人 in Chinese, which literally translates into "Kee-meow-per", and it's a homophone of Keeper in Chinese. but we had to improvise a little so it looks like a typo. 
The typo thing may not be obvious enough at the moment. Perhaps we should make it more obvious by adding more typos, and make her use simpler expressions throughout her messages?

I think more typos on the longer words for all texts would make it more obvious! I actually deleted my comment about Jenkin not  being good at spelling because i went into the game to double check, and most of the texts were perfect spelling

(Except for the chat you get after completing Lily's psyche deepdive, but I saw it after i deleted my comment...)

Ahh alright! I got a bit confused if it was supposed to be misspelled on purpose or not because she spells "Keeper" in the other chats I've seen from her, but I wanted to put it down just in case. Thanks for clearing up the confusion!

yeah it doesn't really feel intentional imo, misspelling something she would presumably by typing quite often and in a way that doesn't really feel like a way you'd actually misspell a word imo, it looks more like mtl than a mistake an english-speaker would make to me. in the first place, the "e" character is the "default" text representation of the "ii" sound
on another note, should Brown be referred to as an "it"? sounds a bit strange to me considering he? is jenkin's long-time partner
maybe Keyper would work better as an intentional typo?

in 7-12, the 50%+ shield achievement needs to be done in the final battle, otherwise the reward doesn't trigger. I'm not sure if the original chinese does mention the final battle condition, but it's not there in english
Brown is referred to as a "her" in Jenkin's voicelines
should be "does not" not "do not", unless if it's multiple deities
"makes a glass of cocktail" is a bit clunky, maybe something like "pours a cocktail"? or even "makes a cocktail"
